Can you believe it's already almost
This year flew by. Since it's almost New
Year's, instead of reflecting on my week
like I normally do, this time I'm going
to reflect on my year. A lot happened
this year. It was full of ups and downs,
but I have so much to be grateful for.
So, I'm going to start by reflecting on
the highlights or most memorable moments
from my year. First of all, I became an
aunt for the first time in February. My
sister had an adorable baby boy named
Wes. Since I live in Korea, I haven't
gotten to meet him in person yet, but we
video chat as much as possible, and I
already love him so much. Another big
life event this year was moving to
Busousan. I lived in Seoul for the past
10 years, so this was a big change for
me, but it's been a really positive
experience. Of course, I'll always love
Seoul, but Busousan is really starting
to feel like home, and living by the
beach has been amazing. Thanks to you,
some of my biggest accomplishments this
year came from my YouTube channel. Our
community reached 350,000
people. I received a best creator award.
>> And I just published my first book,
which honestly still feels like a dream.
I never ever thought I'd have the
opportunity to do that. So, I'm
extremely grateful to you for making it
possible. It's also been a lot of fun
getting to meet some of you in person at
events or even when I'm out and about
filming. I always get so excited when I
meet you. So, please, if you ever see me
out and about, come say hi. Of course,
this year wasn't perfect and I didn't
meet many of the goals that I set for
myself. First of all, I failed to reach
my book goal again. Every year I tell
myself I'm going to read 50 books and
every year I fail. I love reading and
see it as a form of self-care, which is
why I try to prioritize it. But next
year I need to be a little bit more
realistic and set my goal at 30 or
something like that. Another frustrating
part of this year is that I didn't take
care of my health as much as I wanted
to. Health is really important to me,
but my workouts this year were
inconsistent, and I ordered delivery
food way more than I'd like to admit.
Finally, at the beginning of the year, I
told myself I wanted to become more
financially responsible, setting a
budget, paying off loans, and learning
about investing. But honestly, it felt
overwhelming. So, instead of facing it,
I kind of avoided thinking about it.
That brings me to what this year has
taught me. One big life lesson I learned
this year was to do it scared. I
struggle with self-doubt a lot. I had
some really exciting opportunities this
year, but sometimes I felt like I didn't
deserve them or wondered if I was good
enough. But I realized that so many
people feel this way. So it's important
to push through the fear and the
negative self-t talk. do it scared.
Another lesson I learned is that nothing
is ever perfect. And waiting for perfect
usually just means waiting forever. I'm
a bit of a perfectionist and hold myself
to a really high standard. So, I often
feel like whatever I do has to be
flawless. But of course, that's
impossible. So instead of telling myself
that I need to be perfect, I'm trying to
remind myself that I want to be human.
Everyone makes mistakes. So it's
important to be kind and patient with
yourself and learn from those mistakes
rather than stressing out or getting
anxious trying to avoid them. So, going
into the new year, my resolutions are
actually pretty simple. First of all, I
really want to be able to do a pull-up,
just one. I know it might sound silly,
but I just want to be strong, and
honestly, it just looks really cool.
It's kind of a flex. My second
resolution is to be more consistent with
my Korean studies. I tend to do things
in spurts. So, I'll study extremely hard
for a month or two straight and then
slowly I stop. It's very all or nothing.
But I know that when it comes to
learning a language, consistency is key.
Studying even five or 10 minutes every
day is better than nothing at all. So,
next year I'm going to try really hard
to make it a daily habit. Finally, I
want to have more meetups with you guys.
I'll probably be heading back to the US
sometime next year. So, before I go, I
want to meet as many of you as possible
and just spend some good quality time
together. I think it would be a lot of
fun. If I can make it happen, I'll keep
you posted on the details. What are your
New Year's resolutions? Let me know in
the comments below. I hope you reach all
of your goals and that 2026 is the best
year yet for you. Happy New Year.
